name: Kindergarten Journalism & Media Studies — Semester 1
description: An 18-week, play-based Kindergarten pathway for asking questions, drawing observations, and telling what happened. Lessons are brief, hands-on, flexible, and designed for adult-guided learning.
learningGoals: Build confidence and curiosity with asking questions, drawing observations, and telling what happened.
Communicate learning through talk, drawing, movement, play, or a simple creation.
Practice safe, kind, and responsible participation.

units.title: Wonder and notice: asking questions, drawing observations, and telling what happened
units.essentialQuestion: What can we notice and wonder about asking questions, drawing observations, and telling what happened?
outcomes: I can notice details and share a question about asking questions, drawing observations, and telling what happened.
I can use words, pictures, movement, or materials to communicate my thinking.

lessons.title: Notice and talk: asking questions, drawing observations, and telling what happened
lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about asking questions, drawing observations, and telling what happened through a close look at asking questions, drawing observations, and telling what happened.

activities.title: A close look at asking questions, drawing observations, and telling what happened
activities.learnerAction: With an adult or teacher nearby when needed, the learner participates in a close look at asking questions, drawing observations, and telling what happened, uses words, pictures, movement, or materials to communicate thinking, and cleans up safely.

successCriteria: The learner participates safely and respectfully in asking questions, drawing observations, and telling what happened.
The learner communicates one observation, choice, or question using speech, drawing, movement, or a supported response.
